reviews.review_description:
I reinstalled the game after some time, hoping to find some interesting new features. Unfortunately, I noticed that the automatic food suction function towards the worm's mouth, introduced in an old update, is still present.
Personally, I never appreciated this change: the game, which was already accessible and fun, has become too simple and loses part of its charm. The idea that the food "gets sucked" makes the experience less active, almost as if it were the food that looks for the worm, and not the other way around.

Furthermore, considering how much mixed reality has evolved in recent years, I think it would really be the perfect time to introduce it in this game too.
Being able to move freely in your room, with wall recognition and static occlusion of real objects, would give new life to the gameplay and make it much more immersive.

Years ago I gave 5 stars because I found the basic idea original and well-made. But after the introduction of automatic suction (not deactivatable) and the lack of significant updates, especially regarding mixed reality, I unfortunately have to revise my rating.

This review is not intended to be a negative criticism in itself, but a constructive stimulus. I believe that the development team has the talent and ability to take this game to an even higher level. I really hope to see these improvements in a future update.
Am I missing something? This isn't even up to the standard of worms from the floppy disk era. It's at the level of a three year old making toys "zoom through the air". Don't waste your money.

I absolutely love this game it's only £1 and you get a lot of content but it'd be so much better if there was a passthrough map or something as I find myself hitting my surroundings alot more that other games.

I never keep games. I played this five minutes and immediately got two more copies for friends. 

Get in the main store!!

Update: Played with friends and there are issues. Easy to connect to a custom room (like walkabout), gameplay is fun. However, 

1. There is overlapping music when starting and dying -- cut or pause one when playing a new one!

2. Music is so loud we can't talk. It is cool music so give us a volume slider please!

3. The player list won't scroll for me.

Basically this game is gud but needs some basic quality of life updates.
This takes me back, I used to play Snake on my first ever phone in 2D/black & white. Now I’m playing in VR with friends! Very chill & so much fun
Such a fun party game, also fun solo.
Best underrated casual game on Meta. Can’t believe this wasn’t available on the store sooner.
Imagine not wanting to be a slithery little snake

At first look you might browse by this one, but make no mistake. This is a hella-fun game! Addictive gameplay that makes you want to play until your arms are tired! This new take on the old school game "Snake" is a perfect fit for VR and a nice change of pace from shooting stuff! Highly recommended!!

Enjoyed the game it is fun. Just a few things. 

How do u know when the level ends or how to get the 3 stars it just keeps going. 

Would be good if u added power ups in an arcade mode. Shield maybe or slow down the other players. 

Please check out my video. Space Slurpies | Oculus Quest | Applab | Gameplay | Old Nokia Snake in VR https://youtu.be/y1cnXz0l2vU

I love it! I've played about a couple of hours of it now, and it might be simple, but addicting. It requires a lot of room to be able to eat all of the blocks, so make sure to clear out any stuff that may collide with your controllers.

This game is worth wayyy more than the price tag! Endless fun of snake meets slither meets ribbon dancing. Its exciting, nerve wracking, customisable and smooth gameplay leaves nothing to the imagination. Starcade arcade have taken elements to make its an amazing game to play alone or with friends…. And of course DJ Inkers throwing out those mad beats in the menu. Go buy it now and see for yourself.

I don’t think people understand just how addictive this game can be. You start off by “trying it out”, then an hour later you’re building up a sweat, arm burning, but you can’t stop! You’re are tripping over your dog trying to snag all the edibles for yourself without eating your enormous body!! (You’ll have to play to understand) It’s zen, it’s fun, it’s catchy and the devs are awesome to follow on social media as well! Well deserved claps for this game.

reviews.review_title: Lots of fun in quick bursts
reviews.review_description:
Came for the bhaptics support (which is great btw), stayed for the fun gameplay. There are games that need half hour tutorials, and for which you need to reserve an evening to play. This is not one of these games. It's very easy to grasp, and you can always just jump in for a 5-minute-game. It runs smoothly and the transfer of Snake into 3D and VR works surprisingly well. I cannot wait for this to get off the ground and the attention of more people so the multiplayer games get populated.

Oh, also: Absolutely kid-friendly. There are few games I would let my kids play, but this one is at the top of the list.

Given the price, this is probably the best money I have spent on a game in the Oculus store. High praise, yes, but very true.

Let’s start out by stating the most important Space Slurpies is a VR version of the popular worm genre games that came preloaded on all your old school Nokia phones.  You begin playing as a small color customizable Slurpie (worm) and by navigating around the map you gobble up food to grow both armor and larger in size.  Joining you on the map are three other Slurpies (AI or Human). Race to the food and too grow, the Slurpie that last the longest wins.    

If you are a fan of Snake games this is a grab for sure.  There are over 30 levels to unlock in solo mode but are fully unlocked in Zen mode that provide a nice change of visuals while you game.  The games soundtrack has some grove too it.  If you stay in the menu too long you will no doubt find your head bouncing as you pick your next map. When I started playing I said I was just going to give this game a quick try as I am not the biggest fan of “snake games” and it didn’t feel long at all before I got the notification that my headset was dying.  

A great escape from the plethora of FPS, Zombie Bashers, and Music Games that load all of our libraries.  Also bonus points for being a solid game for new VR users.  This is a safe slower paced game to get a new user used to movement and in my personal experience caused no motion sickness.

For more check out my review 
TikTok - @averagepothead

reviews.review_title: Amazing and the music is a banger lol
reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love this game! The menu music is enough to justify buying this game lol. I get it stuck in my head sometimes. I love to play this game when I need to focus and forget my daily life struggles. Really easy to get immersed and I’ve smacked a few things as I got too excited… please use your guardian.

It’s one of those games you’ll probably play in short bursts but it’s perfect that way! I like having Quest games I can pick up and easily play for 15mins on a work break. 

For the future: Would of course always love to see more content added and would happily pay for DLC. Maybe even larger online lobbies if possible? A Slurpies battle royale would be hilarious chaos. But honestly it’s very solid the way it is and it’s fine if it stays the way it is.

It’s one of those games I always recommend to my friends who get headsets. You really can’t go wrong buying this, especially for the price. Also bonus if you follow the devs on social media they are hilarious!

This game is great for anyone looking to get those Slither.io vibes. Do not be fooled though as this is no longer a 2D plane with a mouse to move. You are now in a 3D space and move the slurpie with your hand. Play a game with 3 AI or a group of friends. as of now, the Max lobby size is 4. This is a fun and relaxing game or a competitive masterpiece to outmaneuver your friends.

Space Slurpies is like seeing centipede or snake in VR using your environment around you. It's a great game for only $7! 
https://youtu.be/n1ptdOlb-eI

So space slurpies is basically multiplayer centipedes in vr where you're going in this 3d space trying to collect these icons these dot whatever these things are these little glowing bulb things i don't know but you're trying to collect them and get points and in the process you're getting longer. 

Your goal is to basically trap your opponents or cause them to run into themselves which eventually seems to happen at least with the computer I honestly find this game to be a massive amount of fun it's one of those things that it's so hard to sell it from the gameplay but when you get in the headset it's such a win.

 It also has this green screen thing which allows for some pretty hilarious editing but for me space slurpies is just about having fun it's on app lab it's on steam I've played both versions and I've actually included gameplay of both so if you see the graphical drop down a bit that's that's the reasoning but it's so barely noticeable between the two of them i really don't think it matters that much. I also will say that there is a bit of like cross-play thing where it seems like the versions can't play together which kind of sucks.

In conclusion space slurpies is just so much fun I highly recommend this game I would say this is one that you should definitely try out before you disregard it and also just a quick thank you to the developers for the key.

Really nice, chill game to play. Gameplay is pretty smooth, it does what it does well.

Clicking on the menu items can be a bit glitchy, and sadly there's just not anyone in a multiplayer room do haven't tried that yet.

But the solo mode is great. Looking forward to playing more, and hope more ppl are in the multi player someday

developer_response.body: You're completely right! We've worked to improve the menu items, but if it still needs work, please hit us up on Discord! Thank you for the feedback

reviews.review_description:
Mind blowing Worm game with huge space needed!

You need to have huge space to play this and you need to be fast eating different kind of objects!

Also you could customize your worm by colour etc. There is multiplayer too in this awesome 3D worm game!

You control worm with waving whole controller in air. It's very easy but for first timer you can easily smash into yourself and it's game over!

Easily recommend this game for all people because it's easy to pick up and play short amount of times!
